title: Sweet and Chewy Gumdrop Glory Day
story: Colorful gumdrops take center stage every February 15 on National Gumdrop Day! These tiny, sugar-coated candies have been making people smile for over a century. Today, candy lovers celebrate by munching on their favorite flavors, from classic orange and cherry to wild grape and lemon. Some people use gumdrops to decorate gingerbread houses or create edible art projects. Bakeries often feature gumdrop cookies and cupcakes with these chewy gems on top. Social media fills with rainbow photos of gumdrop collections, and candy stores offer special deals on bulk bags. The best part? You can celebrate this sweet holiday by simply popping a few gumdrops in your mouth and enjoying that perfect chewy texture that makes them so special!
activity_question: What do some people use gumdrops for besides eating them plain?
activity_choices: Decorating gingerbread houses and making edible art
Using them as colorful dice for games
activity_answer: Decorating gingerbread houses and making edible art
bonus_fact: The first gumdrops were made in the 1800s and were originally called "gum drops."

title: Singles Awareness Day: Flying Solo and Loving It!
story: February 15 is Singles Awareness Day, when people who aren't in romantic relationships celebrate being awesome on their own! While couples recover from Valentine's Day, singles throw parties, treat themselves to fancy dinners, buy discounted chocolate, and post fun selfies with hashtags like #SingleAndProud. Some friends get together for movie nights or spa days, proving you don't need a valentine to have an amazing time!
activity_question: What do people often buy on Singles Awareness Day?
follow_up_question: What's your favorite way to celebrate friendship or treat yourself?

title: Hippo Hooray for the Heaviest Day!
story: Every February 15, people around the world celebrate National Hippo Day by sharing their love for these massive, amazing animals! Zoos host special hippo talks and feeding demonstrations, while kids learn incredible facts like how hippos can hold their breath underwater for five minutes. Social media fills with cute hippo videos and photos as families visit aquariums to watch these giants splash around. Many people donate to hippo conservation groups or adopt a hippo through wildlife organizations. Schools often plan hippo-themed activities, teaching students that despite their chunky appearance, hippos can run 30 miles per hour on land! It's the perfect day to appreciate one of Africa's most powerful creatures.
activity_question: How fast can hippos run on land?
bonus_fact: Hippos spend up to 16 hours a day soaking in water to keep their skin from drying out!

title: Maple Leaf Pride Day
story: Canadians wave their red and white flags extra proudly on February 15 for National Flag Day! This special day celebrates Canada's iconic maple leaf flag, which became the country's official symbol decades ago. People hang flags outside their homes, schools display them in classrooms, and many Canadians wear red and white clothing to show their patriotic spirit. Some communities hold flag-raising ceremonies where everyone gathers to sing the national anthem together.
activity_question: What do many Canadians wear on National Flag Day to show their patriotic spirit?
bonus_fact: The maple leaf flag replaced Canada's previous flag that included Britain's Union Jack.

title: Susan B. Anthony: Voting Rights Hero Day
story: Every February 15, Americans honor Susan B. Anthony, a brave woman who fought for women's right to vote. She was arrested in 1872 for voting when only men could! Today, schools teach about her courage, and many people visit her grave in New York to leave "I Voted" stickers as thank-you gifts.
activity_question: What do people often leave at Susan B. Anthony's grave to honor her?
follow_up_question: What other heroes fought for rights that we have today?

title: Wisconsin's Cheesy Day of Fame
story: Every February 15, cheese lovers across America celebrate National Wisconsin Day! People honor the state famous for making over 600 types of cheese. Fans share photos of cheese curds, post about their favorite Wisconsin cheddar, and some even wear cheesehead hats like Green Bay Packers fans. Restaurants serve Wisconsin-style dishes and dairy treats to mark the day.
activity_question: How many types of cheese is Wisconsin famous for making?
bonus_fact: Wisconsin produces about 26% of all cheese made in America!

title: Sweet Dreams Are Made of Butterscotch
story: Every February 15, candy lovers across America celebrate National I Want Butterscotch Day by indulging in this golden, buttery treat! People raid candy stores for butterscotch hard candies, whip up homemade butterscotch pudding, and drizzle butterscotch sauce over ice cream sundaes. Social media fills with photos of butterscotch cookies, candies, and desserts as fans share their favorite recipes. Some bakeries even create special butterscotch cupcakes and pies just for this sweet holiday. It's the perfect excuse to satisfy that butterscotch craving you've been having all year!
activity_question: What do people commonly drizzle butterscotch sauce over on this holiday?
bonus_fact: Butterscotch gets its name from the process of 'scotching' or scoring the candy while it cools.

title: Singapore's Super Defense Day
story: Every February 15, Singapore celebrates Total Defense Day by teaching everyone how to protect their country together! Schools practice emergency drills, families learn about food security, and communities work on cyber safety. It's not just about soldiers - it's about regular people like students, teachers, and parents all doing their part to keep Singapore strong and safe.
activity_question: What do schools do on Total Defense Day in Singapore?
activity_choices: Go on field trips to the zoo
follow_up_question: How could kids in your community help keep everyone safe?
